title: Botswana by Drone - An Aerial Celebration of Botswana's Wildlife and Wilderness.
creator: Martin Harvey
award_or_showcase_provenance: Siena Drone Photo Awards (droneawards.photo) -- Video category, Highly Commended, 2020 edition
notes: MANUALLY CURATED WORKED EXAMPLE -- one exemplar only, per spec AC3.4/Scope-out (no automated scraping/bulk downloading was performed to produce this record). ASSIGNMENT: A Drone Photography Awards (dronephotographyawards.com) winner or finalist in a wildlife or animals category. IMPORTANT DOMAIN CAVEAT: this session verified via `curl` that the literal domain dronephotographyawards.com is NOT a real, active awards body -- it resolves (HTTP 200) but its content is a GoDaddy 'parking-lander' placeholder page (img1.wsimg.com parking-lander assets, WHOIS shows no distinguishing registrant info beyond registry defaults), i.e. a parked/unused domain, not a genuine competition site. No real organization operating at that exact domain could be found via web search this session. This record instead uses the real, independently verifiable 'Siena Drone Photo Awards' / 'Drone Photo Awards' competition (official site droneawards.photo, run by Siena Awards/Siena International Photo Awards), which multiple mainstream outlets (e.g. Matador Network's headline 'The Siena Drone Photography Awards Show How Beautiful Our Planet Is From Above') refer to using almost exactly the assignment's phrase 'Drone Photography Awards'. Treat the source competition identity as a reasonable, disclosed substitution for the non-existent named domain, not a literal match to dronephotographyawards.com. Verified this session via a cookie-jar `curl` session against droneawards.photo/gallery/2020/video (the site issues session cookies and redirect-loops without them): the fetched HTML contains a text block reading 'Botswana by Drone' / 'by Martin Harvey' / rating 'Highly commended' / synopsis 'If you are looking for some of the last true wilderness in Africa then Botswana is the place to go. Enjoy this aerial cinematic celebration of the Okavango Delta and the Makgadikgadi Pans. All filmed with the DJI Phantom 4 Pro drone...' -- confirming this exact title/creator/rating appears on the Drone Photo Awards' own 2020 Video-category results page. Martin Harvey's own profile page on the same site (droneawards.photo/profile/28417/martin-harvey, fetched this session) independently corroborates him as a genuine, established wildlife photographer ('Martin has received numerous photographic awards and commendations in both the BBC Wildlife Photographer of the Year and Agfa Wildlife Awards') who specializes in African wildlife/safari subjects, and links to his own Vimeo channel 'Martin Harvey Wildlife and Travel Photography and Videography'. `source_url` was verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=...&format=json), which returned the exact title 'Botswana by Drone - An Aerial Celebration of Botswana's Wildlife and Wilderness.' (near-verbatim match to the contest listing's title/synopsis) on a channel plausibly operated by Martin Harvey (author_name 'Africa Luxury Travel and Wildlife', author_url https://www.youtube.com/@TheMartinHarvey) -- consistent with his known specialization in Botswana safari/wildlife content. HONEST CAVEAT ON EXACT EMBED-ID PAIRING: the awards site's raw HTML for this gallery row shows a Vimeo iframe (player.vimeo.com/video/420937537) directly adjacent to the 'Botswana by Drone' text block, not a YouTube iframe -- this session's DOM parsing could not mechanically prove that YouTube ID KJmhon6vHTI is pixel-for-pixel the same upload embedded in that specific gallery row (as opposed to, e.g., a separate cross-posted upload of the same film to YouTube). Given the near-identical title wording, matching creator name, matching Okavango Delta/Makgadikgadi Pans/DJI Phantom 4 Pro subject matter, and the channel's clear Botswana-wildlife branding, this is treated as high-confidence corroboration that this is the same work, not proof of byte-identical embed-ID matching -- flagged here per this project's grounding rules rather than presented as fully mechanically confirmed. CATEGORY-ASSIGNMENT NOTE: like this pack's other exemplars, the award is technically won in the contest's genre-based 'Video' category (not a subject-based 'Wildlife'/'Animals' category, which at this competition applies only to still photography) -- however the video's own title ('An Aerial Celebration of Botswana's Wildlife and Wilderness'), synopsis, and the Okavango Delta/Makgadikgadi Pans setting make its wildlife/animals subject matter unambiguous and substantially stronger than a mere landscape/travel piece. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': this session fetched the raw YouTube watch-page HTML for this URL via curl and grepped for 'Creative Commons' markers, finding none, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number, this field is left unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments based on the video's known genre, professional DJI Phantom 4 Pro cinematography, and 'Highly Commended' (not top-prize) placement, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the same no-bulk-download constraint. `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.75, exposure=0.74, motion_smoothness=0.78, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (weights_version 'default-v1', equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python`, yielding composite_score=0.7566666666666666 (rounded to 0.7567 here) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its inputs are estimates rather than measurements.
